cit.basis: Finance Law 2025 (Loi de Finances 2025) raised the standard CIT rate from 15% to 20%, applicable to FY2024 profits declared in 2025 onwards.

fl2025_rate_change.note: Finance Law 2025 rate raise: standard 15% -> 20%; banks, financial institutions and insurance 35% -> 40%.

cit.reduced_regime_note: The 10% reduced regime covers agriculture and fishing, regional development zones, and support activities.

conjunctural_contribution.note: Recent finance laws have imposed a temporary conjunctural contribution (contribution conjoncturelle) on certain large companies, notably banks and financial institutions, on top of CIT. Out of scope for this SME estimator.

cit.note: Administered by the Ministere des Finances / Direction Generale des Impots.

vat.note: Standard VAT (TVA) rate of 19%, with reduced rates of 13% and 7% for specific goods and services.

official_sources.name: Ministere des Finances — Direction Generale des Impots

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; the Finance Law 2025 rates apply to FY2024 profits declared in 2025 onwards.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Minimum tax (impot minimum), conjunctural contributions, export/offshore regimes, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and a local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
